page: Amazon Web Services m8g.large is a general-purpose server instance powered by the custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Built on the 64-bit ARM (arm64) architecture, this instance provides 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. It features a hardware configuration with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It lacks hyper-threading and advanced vector extensions (AVX/AMX) but supports hardware-accelerated AES cryptography.

Benchmark results show a contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores fall into the average tier, while multi-core metrics, such as the Geekbench multi-core score and stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, are categorized as weak. Memory bandwidth tests indicate an average performance of 56.21 GB/sec for large block read-only operations, though small block cached reads and large block write operations are weak. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression stands out as a strong performer at 14.17 GB/sec, whereas multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation with Clang are weak.

The m8g.large instance does not include local NVMe storage and relies on network-attached storage. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps and runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and average single-core capabilities, the instance is suited for general-purpose workloads such as basic database operations, text processing, and lightweight web serving. However, its poor performance in Passmark CPU encryption, extended instructions, and LLM inference tasks makes it less suitable for intensive cryptographic operations, heavy parallel processing, or machine learning workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services m8g.large is an arm64 general-purpose instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 2 physical cores powered by an AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 8.0 GB of memory, providing 4.0 GB per core, and lacks local storage, relying on a 1 Gbps baseline network connection. Performance benchmarks reveal average single-core execution and text processing capabilities, alongside strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core performance, memory write bandwidth, and cryptographic execution are weak to poor. Lacking hyper-threading and advanced vector extensions, this Nitro-based instance is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, basic database operations, and text processing, rather than compute-intensive or highly parallelized applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.large is an arm64 general-purpose instance featuring 2 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a 1 Gbps network baseline, suited for lightweight database and text processing.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.large is an arm64 general-purpose instance with 2 dedicated Graviton vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 1 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores and balanced memory for lightweight database operations and text processing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 2 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 arm64 cores at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and 36 MB of L3 cache
Features a 1 Gbps baseline network connection with no local storage
Delivers strong single-threaded decompression but weak multi-core scaling
Suited for general-purpose workloads, text processing, and basic database tasks
